URL: https://gehu.ac.in/dehradun/pharmacy/
TITLE: Pharmacy Colleges in Dehradun, Uttarakhand

- Admission Apply Now Scholarships Academic Fee Structure Academic Calendar Hostel Fee Brochure
- Academics Department of Computer Science and Engineering Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering Department of Mechanical Engineering Department of Civil Engineering School of Management Department of Computer Application Department of English School of Commerce B.Voc in Accounting & Finance Allied Sciences School of Agriculture Law Design
- Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering
- The School of Pharmacy at Graphic Era Hill University (GEHU) is the Best Pharmacy College in Dehradun, Uttarakhand. It is committed to providing students with a world-class education and preparing them for a career in the pharmaceutical industry.
- Graphic Era Hill University has advanced facilities and equipment that enable students to gain practical knowledge and experience in the field of Pharmacy. The faculty members are highly qualified and experienced in their respective fields, providing students with the necessary guidance and support to succeed in their academic and professional pursuits.
- One of the unique aspects of the School of Pharmacy at GEHU is its focus on research and innovation. The School encourages students and faculty members to engage in research activities and provides them with the necessary resources to conduct cutting-edge research in the field of Pharmacy. The School also collaborates with leading pharmaceutical companies and research institutions to promote research and innovation in the field of Pharmacy.
- The School offers a distinguished undergraduate program in Pharmacy. The Bachelor of Pharmacy (B. Pharma) program is a four-year undergraduate program that provides students with a comprehensive understanding of the principles of Pharmacy and prepares them for a career in the pharmaceutical industry.
- The School also has a well-equipped pharmacy practice lab where students learn the practical aspects of Pharmacy. The lab is designed to provide students with hands-on experience in dispensing, compounding, and preparing various pharmaceutical products.
- Apart from academic programs, the School also conducts various workshops, seminars, and guest lectures by eminent personalities from the pharmaceutical industry. These events provide students with the opportunity to interact with experts in the field of Pharmacy and gain insights into the latest developments and trends in the industry.
- The School of Pharmacy at Graphic Era Hill University also emphasizes industry-academia collaboration. The School has various collaborations that provide students with the opportunity to gain practical experience and exposure to the latest technologies and innovations in the industry.
- The School of Pharmacy at Graphic Era Hill University is a world-class institution that provides students with a comprehensive education in the field of Pharmacy. With its focus on research and innovation, state-of-the-art facilities, and experienced faculty, the School prepares students for a successful career in the pharmaceutical industry.
- School of Pharmacy Graphic Era Hill University Bell Road, Clement Town Dehradun, Uttarakhand

- Department of Mass and Media Communication – Where Stories Shape the Future
- Welcome to the Department of Mass and Media Communication at Graphic Era Hill University, a hub for creativity, innovation, and industry-focused education. Our department focuses on equipping future media professionals with the skills, ethics, and technological expertise required to succeed in today’s rapidly evolving world of Journalism, Digital Media, Advertising, and Public Relations. With an evolving media landscape, we educate our students for today, for tomorrow—where technology meets storytelling, and content shapes society.
- The Department of Mass and Media Communication at Graphic Era Hill University has consistently provided high-quality education, research, and industry exposure to students aiming to build a successful career in media and communication. Over the years, the department has nurtured alumni who have gone on to become successful journalists, photographers, editors, event managers, radio jockey, entrepreneurs, influencers, and researchers in reputed organizations and platforms across India and beyond.
- The department is supported by a team of highly qualified faculty members and seasoned media professionals who bring in-depth academic knowledge and hands-on industry experience. Our faculty members have worked across national and international media organizations and hold expertise in fields like journalism, digital media, advertising, film production, media research, and strategic communication. The programs offered in Mass and Media Communication aim to equip students with essential communication skills, media ethics, production knowledge, and storytelling techniques needed in today’s fast-evolving media landscape.
- In addition to our undergraduate and postgraduate programs, the Department of Mass and Media Communication also offers a rigorous PhD program aimed at fostering advanced research in diverse areas of journalism, new media, broadcast media, and others. The program is supported by experienced faculty mentors and access to extensive research resources, encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration and scholarly excellence.
- Our undergraduate, postgraduate, and PhD programs in Journalism and Mass Communication provide students with holistic exposure to the world of media. - B.Tech CSE is an undergraduate degree program that focuses on the study of computer software, hardware, and networking, preparing students for a career in the field of computer engineering.
- B.Tech CSE is a 4-year undergraduate degree program that focuses on the application and principles of computer science and engineering. Students learn about various aspects of computer science such as software development, programming, data structures, algorithms, computer networks, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and more. The course aims to develop the technical and analytical skills of students to prepare them for various careers in the field of computer science. Additionally, the course provides students with practical experience through internships, projects, and research, which can further enhance their job prospects. - Launch your career with our B.Tech Computer Science Engineering program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination with Physics, Mathematics and Chemistry or equivalent or as per AICTE guidelines with not less than 50% marks either in PCM or aggregate.
- 1. Curriculum: The curriculum of B.Tech CSE covers a wide range of topics related to computer science and engineering. The early semesters are usually common for all engineering disciplines and involve foundational courses such as mathematics, physics, and engineering principles. In the subsequent years, students study core CSE subjects such as programming languages, data structures, algorithms, computer networks, operating systems, database management systems, software engineering, and more. Students also have the opportunity to choose from a range of electives based on their interests and career goals. This prepares them for careers in the industry as well as higher studies in computer science and related fields.
- 2. Practical training: In addition to theoretical knowledge, the program also provides practical training through lab work, projects, and internships. This allows students to gain hands-on experience and apply their knowledge to real-world problems.
- 3. Industry-relevant skills: The B.Tech CSE program is designed to equip students with industry-relevant skills that are in high demand in the technology sector. Graduates of the program are well-prepared for jobs in software development, data analysis, cybersecurity, and other related fields.
- 4. Research opportunities: The B.Tech CSE program also provides opportunities for students to pursue research in the field of computer science and engineering. This can lead to advanced degrees such as a Master’s or Ph.D. and a career in academia or research.
- 5. Flexibility in Specialization: Students have the flexibility to choose a specialization within the program, such as artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, data science, software engineering, or web technologies, based on their interests and career goals.
- B.Tech CSE graduates have excellent job prospects in various sectors, including software development, information technology, finance, healthcare, education, and government.
- 1. Software Engineers: Professionals who design, develop, test, and maintain software applications using programming languages and software development methodologies.
- 2. System Analysts: Experts who analyze business processes, design and evaluate computer systems, and recommend technology solutions to meet specific business needs.
- 3. Software Developers: Professionals who write, modify, and maintain software code, and develop new software applications using various programming languages and tools.
- 4. System Scientists: Experts who analyze complex systems, identify problems, and develop solutions using mathematical and scientific principles.
- 5. System Engineers: Professionals who design, implement, and manage complex systems that integrate hardware, software, and network technologies to meet specific business needs.
- 6. IT Engineers: Experts who design, deploy, and maintain information technology systems, networks, and infrastructure to support organizational goals and objectives.

- What is the difference between B.Tech CSE and BSC CS?
- B.Tech CSE is a four-year engineering program that emphasizes the application of engineering principles to computer systems and software development. BSC CS, on the other hand, is a three-year science degree program that deals with the theoretical foundations of computation and information processing. B.Tech CSE is more practical and engaging, while BSC CS is more detail oriented.

- B.Tech CSE is a professional engineering degree that allows students to become licensed engineers, providing higher salaries and job opportunities. The program encourages innovation and entrepreneurship, providing exposure to various technologies.

- Additionally, students can opt for higher studies or research in top universities abroad. The international exposure and experience gained during the program also increase the chances of getting a job abroad.

- B.Tech CSE (Hons.) with specialization in Cybersecurity is an undergraduate program that combines computer science principles and techniques with cybersecurity concepts to prepare students for careers in the rapidly growing field of cybersecurity.
- B.Tech CSE (Hons.) with specialization in Cybersecurity is a four-year undergraduate degree program that aims to provide students with in-depth knowledge and expertise in the field of cybersecurity. This program combines the principles of computer science, software development, and cybersecurity to prepare students for careers in various industries such as government agencies, financial institutions, healthcare organizations, and technology company.
- Launch your career with our Engineering program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination with Physics, Mathematics and Chemistry or equivalent or as per AICTE guidelines with not less than 50% marks either in PCM or aggregate.
- The course curriculum for B.Tech CSE (Hons.) with a specialization in Cybersecurity covers a wide range of topics, including computer programming, algorithms, data structures, computer networks, operating systems, database management, and cybersecurity.
- Students are also introduced to specialized courses such as ethical hacking, cryptography, digital forensics, network security, and cyber law. The early semesters of the program focus on building a durable foundation in computer science and programming fundamentals.
- Students are introduced to programming languages such as Java, C, C++, and Python, and learn about data structures, algorithms, and computer networks. They also gain an understanding of the theoretical aspects of computer science such as automata theory, formal languages, and computational complexity.
- Students continue to develop their programming and software development skills by learning about operating systems, database management systems, and web technologies.
- They also gain an understanding of computer security principles and practices, including threat modeling, risk assessment, and vulnerability analysis. In the later semesters of the program, students focus on specialized courses in cybersecurity. They learn about ethical hacking techniques, digital forensics, network security, and cryptography.
- They also gain an understanding of cybersecurity laws and regulations and the legal and ethical implications of cybersecurity breaches. Students work on projects related to cybersecurity.
- They apply the knowledge and skills they have acquired throughout the program to develop solutions for real-world cybersecurity problems.
- They also have the opportunity to undertake internships with cybersecurity companies or government agencies to gain hands-on experience in the field.
- Cybersecurity offers a wide range of career opportunities, including security analysts who monitor and analyze security threats, security consultants who help clients develop security strategies, penetration testers who test computer systems for vulnerabilities, security engineers who design and implement security solutions, cryptographers who encrypt and decrypt data, cybersecurity managers who oversee the security of an organization’s computer systems and networks, forensic analysts who investigate security breaches, ethical hackers who work with government agencies, and chief information security officers who are responsible for an organization’s overall security strategy. With the increasing importance of digital security, there is a growing demand for skilled professionals in this field.
- What is the scope of B.Tech CSE (Cybersecurity) in India?
- B.Tech CSE (Cybersecurity) is an undergraduate degree program that focuses on developing skills in cybersecurity, computer science, and programming. Graduates in this field can find job opportunities in various sectors such as IT security firms, government organizations, financial institutions, healthcare, and e-commerce companies.

- B.Tech CSE (Cybersecurity) provides a strong foundation in cybersecurity principles, network security, cryptography, and computer science. It equips students with the skills to identify and prevent cyber threats, secure network infrastructure, and protect data from cyber-attacks. Moreover, it offers numerous opportunities to work with leading IT security companies, research institutions, and government organizations.

Some of the common cybersecurity threats include malware attacks, phishing, ransomware, and social engineering. B.Tech CSE (Cybersecurity) graduates can help address these threats by developing and implementing effective security measures, conducting regular security audits, and providing training and awareness to end-users.

- The University has an IoT and Cisco lab, which has various CISCO computer systems installed with the updated softwares, which are specialized systems for networking and Internet of Things.
- Well-furnished classrooms and lecture theatres with projector
- The University has well-furnished and adequate classrooms and lecture theatres with projector, CCTV camera and adequate infrastructure for students.
- The students have access to the Internet facility 24×7 throughout the university.
- The University has a well-equipped library, having almost all the books in all the domains with multiple copies of each book, so that the books are available for all the students. - B. Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering
- B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering is a four-year undergraduate program that focuses on the principles, design, and development of modern communication systems and electronic devices. The curriculum covers a wide range of topics such as analog and digital communication, microprocessors, embedded systems, digital signal processing, and wireless communication technologies.
- The program is designed to provide students with a balanced mix of theoretical knowledge and practical skills. Students explore the underlying principles of electronics and communication engineering and apply them through hands-on laboratory sessions, real-time projects, and industry-oriented assignments. Industrial visits, seminars, and expert lectures are integrated into the curriculum to ensure students gain real-world exposure and insight into current industry practices.
- By incorporating the latest trends and technological advancements, the program prepares students for the dynamic and fast-paced electronics and communication industry. Graduates are equipped to take on challenges in core areas like communication networks, embedded technologies, VLSI design, IoT systems, and automation, making them future-ready professionals with strong problem-solving and innovation skills.
- Launch your career with our B. Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Based on merit prepared from the qualifying examination.
- Seats are also allocated based on projected scores in Class 12, calculated using Class 10th performance — applicable for meritorious candidates.
- Candidates must have passed Class XII from a recognized board with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics, or as per AICTE guidelines, with a minimum of 50% marks either in PCM or overall aggregate.
- B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering at GEHU is a comprehensive program that blends theoretical concepts with practical implementation. The curriculum covers:
- Electromagnetic Field Theory (EMFT): Understanding how electromagnetic fields behave in different materials and environments.
- Microcontrollers and Embedded Systems: Designing and programming embedded systems used in automation, smart devices, and IoT applications.
- VLSI Technology and Design: Learning how to design very-large-scale integration circuits for high-performance electronic devices.
- Digital Signal Processing & Microwave Engineering: Key technologies used in radar, satellite communication, and signal enhancement systems.
- Analog Integrated Circuits & Communication Systems: Core fundamentals behind the development of audio, video, and wireless transmission technologies.
- Alongside classroom learning, the program includes:
- Extensive lab sessions using advanced tools and industry-grade software
- Minor and major projects to develop engineering acumen
- Industrial visits and workshops for real-world exposure
- Opportunities for research, internships, and skill development certifications
- Graduates are also well-prepared to pursue advanced studies in electronics, robotics, embedded systems, and communication networks.
- B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering opens up a wide array of career opportunities in both public and private sectors. With increasing digitization and technological innovation, there is a growing demand for skilled ECE professionals across industries such as:
- Graduates may also pursue higher education such as M.Tech, MBA, or Ph.D. to further specialize and grow in academia or leadership roles.
- What are the career opportunities after completing B.Tech ECE?
- B.Tech ECE graduates have diverse career opportunities across industries including telecommunications, IT, electronics, aerospace, and defence. They can work as engineers, analysts, R&D professionals, or pursue government jobs and higher studies.
- What skills are essential for success in B.Tech ECE?

- What is the difference between a Diploma in Automobile Engineering and a Bachelor's degree in Automobile Engineering?
- The main difference between a Diploma in Automobile Engineering and a Bachelor’s degree in Automobile Engineering lies in the education level and depth of study. A diploma program typically provides foundational knowledge and practical skills within a shorter duration (3 years), while a bachelor’s degree offers an in-depth curriculum spanning four years, including advanced concepts, research opportunities, and broader career prospects.

- BTech Mechanical Engineering is a highly versatile degree with a wide range of career opportunities. Mechanical engineers work in diverse fields ranging from automotive, aerospace, power generation, manufacturing, robotics, and many more. Mechanical engineers are involved in the design, development, and manufacturing of various products, ranging from automobiles to medical devices. They also play a critical role in the development of renewable energy sources and sustainable technologies. Here are some of the key career prospects for BTech Mechanical Engineering graduates:
- Quality Control Engineer: Quality control engineers are responsible for ensuring that products and processes meet quality standards and regulations. They analyze data and make recommendations to improve quality and efficiency.
- Design Engineer: Design engineers are responsible for designing, testing, and evaluating products, equipment, and machinery. This role involves collaborating with other engineers and stakeholders to ensure that products meet specifications, regulations, and quality standards.
- Manufacturing Engineer: Manufacturing engineers are involved in the design, development, and improvement of manufacturing processes and systems. They work closely with other engineers and production teams to ensure that products are produced efficiently and at the highest quality standards.
- Product Development Engineer: Product development engineers work on developing new products or improving existing ones. They are involved in all stages of the product development process, from concept design to manufacturing and testing.
- Research and Development Engineer: Research and development engineers work on developing new technologies and processes. They collaborate with other engineers, scientists, and stakeholders to develop new products, materials, and systems.
- What skills are required for a successful career in mechanical engineering?
- To have a successful career in mechanical engineering, individuals need to have strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills. They should possess creativity and communication skills, technical proficiency, and teamwork abilities. They must be able to work on multiple projects simultaneously and prioritize their workload efficiently. Additionally, a solid understanding of the principles of mechanics, energy, and materials science is essential.
- How does BTech Mechanical Engineering differ from other engineering streams?
- BTech Mechanical Engineering is different from other engineering streams in its focus on the principles of mechanics, energy, and materials science. Unlike other engineering streams, it covers topics, such as thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and machine design, which are essential for developing machines and devices. Graduates from this program have various career options, including design engineering, production engineering, and project management.
- What are some emerging areas in BTech Mechanical Engineering?
- Some emerging areas in BTech Mechanical Engineering include additive manufacturing, robotics, and renewable energy. Additive manufacturing is revolutionizing the manufacturing industry by allowing for the creation of complex geometries. Robotics is becoming increasingly important in healthcare, manufacturing, and aerospace. Renewable energy is another emerging area, and mechanical engineers are at the forefront of developing more efficient and cost-effective renewable energy technologies.

- Cutting Edge Laboratories & Facilities available with the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Graphic Era Hill University, Dehradun further enhances the learning experience of our students. On account of the world class facilities and faculty, our students are equipped with tools and resources as they move their inspiration into the applications domain. - The state of the art laboratories with the Department of Mechanical Engineering at GEHU include
- Computer Aided Machine Drawing Lab70 work stations to accommodate any high end software. In our department, conventional drawing aids are replaced by drafting software to create the drawing sheets.
- Metallographic & Material Testing LabCommercial Universal Testing Machine of capacity 100 Tonne (Computerised) is functioning to give practical knowledge by preparing specimen testing reports for nearby industries.Camera Fitted microscope with display system is used to study and discuss the minute microstructure on any material. Even a test report can be generated for the specimen. Brinell and Rockwell hardness testing machine, Impact testing machine etc., are available for material testing.
- Foundry & Forging LabAluminum melting furnace is provided to give practical training to the students regarding Casting process. Students are trained to make moulds for their own objects and Cast the metal.Open hearth furnaces are provided to impart practical know how about Forging process. Machine Shop is also provided to the students.
- Applied ThermodynamicsFour stroke, two stroke Petrol and diesel engines are provided with gadgets to note reading regarding their performances. All the experimentation work is practical oriented. Motorized Cut sections are available to understand the working of different equipments and engines.
- Measurement & Metrology LabComplete lab is equipped with high end measuring equipments (Mitutoyo make), along with three granite surface plates of 5 micron finish.Measurements with LVDT, Dynamometers, Load Cell etc is available and in the curriculum of the students.
- Fluid Mechanics and Fluid Machinery LabAll the Turbines, pumps, blowers are available in the form of test rigs, to impart technical training to the students.
- Design LabSoftware “SOLIDEDGE” is used to design and then assemble different components of mechanical use. The students are to make the all necessary calculations regarding designing a component, and then with the help of software they have to draw is in 3D.
- Automation & FMS LabModern computing facilities are available for students at the CAD & Computer Integrated Manufacturing Laboratories. 3D printer is available of modelling and rapid prototyping.
- CNC machining centreThe centre has high end machining centre to impart training of designing and making a component on a CNC machining centre.
- Automobile Engineering LabThe centre has Motorized Cut sections of different assemblies used in automobiles and Car chassis.

Some of the career options include:
- Construction Engineer: A construction engineer oversees the planning and execution of construction projects, ensuring they are completed on time, within budget, and to the required standards.
- Structural Engineer: These people design and analyze the structural elements of buildings, bridges, and other infrastructure.
- Site Engineer: supervise the construction process on-site. They ensure the work is being carried out according to the design specifications and safety standards.
- Urban Planner: An urban planner is responsible for designing and managing the development of urban areas, ensuring that they are sustainable, functional, and aesthetically pleasing.
- Water Resource Engineer: A water resource engineer designs and implements solutions for water management and conservation, such as dams, reservoirs, and irrigation systems.

- What is the difference between BSc in Civil Engineering and Diploma In Civil Engineering?
- The main difference between a BSc in Civil Engineering and a Diploma in Civil Engineering is the level and depth of education. A BSc degree program typically takes four years and provides a more comprehensive education in theoretical concepts and practical applications. A diploma program is usually shorter and focuses more on practical skills and hands-on training.
- What are some of the emerging technologies that students can expect to learn in a Diploma in Civil Engineering program?
- Diploma in Civil Engineering programs can include emerging technologies such as Building Information Modeling (BIM), 3D printing, and drone surveying. Students may also learn about sustainable design and construction techniques, as well as the use of advanced materials like carbon fibre composites. Virtual Reality and Augmented Reality can also be incorporated to simulate real-world scenarios and improve project visualisation.

- B.Tech Civil Engineering is a four-year undergraduate program that focuses on designing, constructing, and maintaining infrastructure such as buildings, roads, bridges, and water systems using principles of mathematics, physics, and technology.
- Civil Engineering is considered one of the oldest and broadest engineering disciplines that require a deep understanding of mathematical, physical, and social sciences. The B.Tech Civil Engineering program provides students with a comprehensive understanding of various engineering concepts, including environmental engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, and structural engineering. The demand for civil engineers is high, as they are required to tackle various infrastructure challenges and ensure that communities have safe and efficient structures and facilities.
- Launch your career with our B.Tech program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination with Physics, Mathematics and Chemistry or equivalent or as per AICTE guidelines with not less than 50% marks either in PCM or aggregate.
- Curriculum: B.Tech Civil Engineering covers various theoretical and foundational courses in mathematics, physics, and chemistry, followed by core courses in subjects such as mechanics, structures, materials, surveying, and geotechnical engineering. Students also take courses in areas such as transportation engineering, water resources engineering, environmental engineering, and project management. The curriculum aims to prepare graduates with the knowledge and skills needed for careers in construction, infrastructure development, and related fields.
- Practical Experience: B.Tech Civil Engineering emphasises on practical experience and hands-on learning. Students participate in laboratory work, fieldwork, and industry internships to gain practical experience in the application of engineering principles.
- Industry-Relevant Skills: The program focuses on developing industry-relevant skills such as project management, communication, problem-solving, and teamwork. These skills are highly valued in the civil engineering industry and are essential for success in the field.

- Specialisations: B.Tech Civil Engineering programs offer specialisations in areas such as structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, environmental engineering, and construction management.
- Below mentioned are some job prospects for graduates of B.Tech Civil Engineering:
- Civil Engineer: Civil engineers design, plan, and oversee construction projects such as buildings, roads, bridges, and water supply systems. They use their knowledge of materials, mechanics, and mathematics to ensure that projects are structurally sound, safe, and cost-effective.
- Structural Engineer: Structural engineers design and analyse the framework of buildings, bridges, and other structures to ensure that they are stable, durable, and safe. They use computer simulations, models, and analysis tools to test designs and identify potential weaknesses.
- Geotechnical Engineer: Geotechnical engineers study the physical properties of soil and rock to determine their stability and suitability for construction projects. They analyse the ground conditions and provide recommendations on the design and construction of structures, such as foundations and retaining walls, to ensure their stability and safety.
- Environmental Engineer: Environmental engineers develop solutions to environmental problems related to water and air pollution, waste management, and environmental sustainability. They work on projects such as designing water treatment systems, assessing environmental impacts of construction projects, and developing sustainable solutions for energy production.
- Are there research opportunities available for B.Tech Civil Engineering students?

- Yes, Graphic Era Hill University organizes industry visits and field trips for B.Tech Civil Engineering students to enhance their practical understanding of real-world construction projects, infrastructure development, and site visits to gain hands-on experience.
- Can B.Tech Civil Engineering students pursue higher studies or specialization after graduation?

- The departmental lecture halls fitted with high-Quality Audio Visual Equipment with overhead LCD projectors creating a platform for trainers to deliver high quality and informative lectures and presentations. Students also use the same facility during the presentation of Term Seminars and thereby develop skills that are superlative among their peer groups.
- The department has well equipped and well developed laboratories. The curriculum also includes training the students in computer aided design and drafting using standard packages like STAAD PRO and CAD etc.
- Fluid Mechanics and Hydraulics and Hydraulic Machine Lab
- The Fluid Mechanics Laboratory in the Civil Engineering Department offers students hands-on experience with fluid behavior and dynamics. Equipped with state-of-the-art apparatus, students can conduct experiments related to fluid flow, pressure measurements, and hydraulic machinery.
- The Geotechnical Engineering Lab provides resources for soil and foundation testing. It houses equipment for soil classification, compaction tests, and permeability assessments. This lab is essential for understanding soil behavior and ensuring safe and sustainable foundation designs.
- In this lab, students explore the properties and behaviors of construction materials. With facilities for mixing, curing, and testing concrete, as well as analyzing the properties of steel, asphalt, and other materials, this lab is crucial for developing durable and sustainable construction practices.
- Our Structural Engineering Lab focuses on the analysis and design of various structural components.
- The Transportation Engineering Lab focuses on the design and analysis of transportation systems. It features pavement testing equipment. This lab aims to improve the efficiency and safety of transportation networks.

- Human Resource Manager: Responsible for recruiting, training, and managing employees, as well as overseeing employee benefits and compensation packages.

- Sales Manager: Leads a team of sales representatives to generate revenue by selling products or services.

- Business Development Manager: Identifies new business opportunities and develops strategies to expand a company’s market share.
- Financial Planner: Helps clients manage their finances by developing customized financial plans that include budgeting, saving, investing, and retirement planning.

- No, it is not necessary to have a background in commerce to pursue a BBA program. - Admission Apply Now Scholarships Academic Fee Structure Academic Calendar Hostel Fee Brochure
- Academics Department of Computer Science and Engineering Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering Department of Mechanical Engineering Department of Civil Engineering School of Management Department of Computer Application Department of English School of Commerce B.Voc in Accounting & Finance Allied Sciences School of Agriculture Law Design
- Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering
- The BCA (Hons.) in Cyber Security program provides a comprehensive curriculum that covers a wide range of critical subjects essential for understanding and combating cyber threats. Students delve into various types of cyber threats, including malware, phishing, and ransomware, and learn effective strategies to counter these threats. The course also includes a detailed study of cryptographic techniques and protocols used to secure communication and protect data integrity.
- The program emphasizes network security, where students explore methods to secure network infrastructure, including the deployment of firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and the implementation of secure communication protocols. A significant portion of the curriculum is dedicated to ethical hacking, offering hands-on experience in penetration testing and ethical hacking, which teaches students to identify vulnerabilities and proactively secure systems. Additionally, the subject of digital forensics focuses on the methods and tools used for investigating cyber incidents, data recovery, and digital evidence analysis.
- The curriculum is designed to ensure that students are not only knowledgeable in theory but also proficient in practical applications. The course includes extensive lab work and project-based learning, allowing students to work on real-world problems and gain experience with the latest cyber security tools and technologies. This practical approach ensures that graduates are well-prepared for the challenges of a career in cyber security, with a solid foundation in both the technical and ethical aspects of the field.
- Launch your career with our BCA (Hons.) in Cyber Security program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination in any stream with not less than 55% marks with mathematics mandatory upto class 12th.
- The BCA (Hons.) in Cyber Security program opens doors to a wide range of career opportunities in both the private and public sectors. Graduates can pursue roles such as:
- Cyber Security Analyst: Professionals in this role analyze and identify security threats and develop strategies to protect organizational data and systems.
- Information Security Manager: They oversee and manage an organization’s security measures, ensuring compliance with security policies and minimizing risks of data breaches.
- Network Security Engineer: These experts are responsible for designing and implementing secure network solutions to protect data integrity and prevent unauthorized access.
- Ethical Hacker: Ethical hackers are employed to test and enhance the security of information systems by identifying and fixing vulnerabilities.
- Digital Forensics Expert: They play a crucial role in investigating cybercrimes, recovering compromised data, and supporting legal investigations with digital evidence.

- Journalist: Graduates can pursue a career in journalism, working for newspapers, magazines, online publications, or broadcast media. They can research and report on current events, conduct interviews, and write news articles and features.

- Teacher/Educator: Graduates can become teachers and educators, sharing their knowledge and passion for English language and literature with others. They can teach at schools, colleges, and language institutes, or pursue further education to become professors.
- Publishing Professional: Graduates can find opportunities in the publishing industry, working as literary agents, book editors, or marketing executives. They can contribute to the production and promotion of books, magazines, and digital content.
- Freelance Writer: Graduates can work as freelance writers, taking on a variety of writing projects for different clients. They can write articles, blog posts, product descriptions, and other types

- The MA English program at Graphic Era Hill University features a well-rounded curriculum that covers a variety of subjects, including:
- Literary Theory and Criticism: Students will learn about different schools of literary theory and criticism, including structuralism, post-structuralism, and deconstruction.
- Creative Writing: The program offers several courses in creative writing, including poetry, fiction, and non-fiction writing. Students will have the opportunity to develop their writing skills and explore different genres and styles.
- Linguistics: Students will learn about the structure and function of language, including phonetics, syntax, and semantics. They will also explore different theories of language acquisition and language change.
- British and American Literature: The program offers a comprehensive survey of British and American literature, from the medieval period to the present day. Students will study major literary movements, such as Romanticism, Modernism, and Postmodernism, and analyze works by major authors like Shakespeare, Austen, and more.

- Collaborative Learning: The program encourages collaborative learning, with students working together on group projects, presentations, and research papers. This not only helps students develop teamwork and communication skills but also fosters a sense of community within the program.
- The course structure includes core courses in English literature, language, and linguistics. Students study a range of subjects such as literary theory, creative writing, cultural studies, and gender studies. The program may also include a research project or thesis. The course structure is designed to develop the critical thinking, research, and writing skills of the students.

Additionally, students may face challenges related to time management, as the program requires a significant amount of reading, writing, and research.

TITLE: B.Com (Hons) – Corporate Public Accountant - GEHU, Dehradun

- Admission Apply Now Scholarships Academic Fee Structure Academic Calendar Hostel Fee Brochure
- Academics Department of Computer Science and Engineering Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering Department of Mechanical Engineering Department of Civil Engineering School of Management Department of Computer Application Department of English School of Commerce B.Voc in Accounting & Finance Allied Sciences School of Agriculture Law Design
- Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering
- The B.Com (Hons) program integrates the prestigious U.S. CPA certification, providing a global gateway to careers in Corporate Finance and beyond. Recognised worldwide as the U.S. equivalent of CA, the CPA qualification is designed to complement BCom (H) degree. The US Certified Public Accountant certification enables global recognition due to its broad acceptance which creates diverse professional chances for auditing, taxation and financial reporting. With this unique program, graduates gain advanced skills in financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and business analysis, aligning financial strategies with organisational goals.
- Corporate Finance lies at the heart of this program, focusing on managing an organisation’s financial resources to maximise shareholder value. Students will develop expertise in areas such as capital budgeting, financial planning, investment analysis, risk management, and corporate governance. By mastering these skills, graduates will be equipped to influence major business decisions, optimise capital structures, and drive long-term profitability.Graduates receive a B.Com / B.Com (Hons) degree along with a globally recognized professional certification, equipping them to excel in the competitive finance sector. - What is the difference between B.Com and B.Com (Hons)?
- The main difference between B.Com and B.Com (Hons) is that the latter is a more specialized and advanced version of the former. B.Com (Hons) offers in-depth knowledge of commerce-related subjects, while B.Com provides a broader overview of commerce. B.Com (Hons) curriculum also includes research projects and presentations, which are not typically part of the B.Com program.

- What are the career prospects after completing the B.Com (Hons) program?
- After completing the B.Com (Hons) program, graduates can pursue various career paths such as accounting, auditing, banking, finance, investment banking, consulting, marketing, and sales. They may work in public or private organizations, government agencies, or non-profit organizations. Some graduates may also choose to pursue further education such as a Master’s in Business Administration (MBA) or Chartered Accountancy (CA) to advance their careers.

- Admission Apply Now Scholarships Academic Fee Structure Academic Calendar Hostel Fee Brochure
- Academics Department of Computer Science and Engineering Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering Department of Mechanical Engineering Department of Civil Engineering School of Management Department of Computer Application Department of English School of Commerce B.Voc in Accounting & Finance Allied Sciences School of Agriculture Law Design
- Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering
- B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance is a four-year undergraduate degree program that provides students with comprehensive knowledge and skills in Banking and Finance, preparing them for various roles in the financial sector.
- B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance is a specialized four-year undergraduate degree program that focuses on providing students with a comprehensive understanding of the banking and finance industry. This program covers a broad range of topics, including financial accounting, economics, financial management, and business law.
- The program is designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills required to excel in various roles within the financial sector, such as investment banking, corporate finance, risk management, and financial analysis.
- The program offers a blend of theoretical knowledge and practical experience, with opportunities for students to participate in internships, case studies, and research projects. Students also learn how to use financial software and tools.
- Apart from the technical skills, the program also emphasizes the development of soft skills, such as communication, teamwork, and leadership. These skills are essential in the financial industry, where collaboration and effective communication are crucial for success.
- Launch your career in Commerce with our B.Com. (Hons.) Banking and Finance program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination in any stream with not less than 50% marks.
- Graduates of the B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance program have a wide range of career opportunities available to them in the financial industry. From investment banking and financial analysis to risk management and wealth management, the program provides a strong foundation of knowledge and skills that can be applied to a diverse range of roles.
- Risk management analyst – Identifies and mitigates potential risks to an organization’s financial health.
- Corporate finance manager – Manages an organization’s financial resources and investments to meet strategic goals.
- Wealth management advisor – Advises clients on how to manage their wealth and invest their assets.
- Fund manager – Manages investment portfolios on behalf of clients or organizations.
- Financial planner – Helps clients plan and manage their financial futures, including retirement and savings goals.
- Credit analyst – Evaluates the creditworthiness of individuals and organizations applying for loans or credit.
- Financial consultant – Provides advice and guidance to clients on various financial matters, including investments and tax planning.
- Stockbroker – Buys and sells stocks and other securities on behalf of clients.
- Insurance underwriter – Evaluates insurance applications and determines the level of risk involved in insuring individuals or organizations.
- Compliance officer – Ensures that an organization complies with all relevant laws and regulations.
- Treasury analyst – Manages an organization’s cash flow and financial risks.
- Loan officer – Evaluates loan applications and makes lending decisions.
- Mergers and acquisitions specialist – Advises clients on mergers, acquisitions, and other corporate transactions.
- Comprehensive Curriculum: The program provides a comprehensive curriculum that covers essential topics in banking, finance, economics, accounting, and business management, giving students a well-rounded understanding of the financial industry.
- Professional Development: The program provides students with opportunities for professional development, such as internships and networking events, which help them, build connections and gain practical experience in the financial industry.
- Industry-Relevant Skills: The program equips students with industry-relevant skills, such as financial analysis, risk management, and investment analysis, that are in high demand by employers in the financial industry.
- Experienced Faculty: The program is taught by experienced faculty who have knowledge and expertise in the financial industry, providing students with valuable insights and guidance.
- What are the career prospects after completing the B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance program?
- B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance program opens up career opportunities in banking, investment management, financial planning, insurance, stockbroking, risk management, consulting, and government jobs. Graduates can work as loan officers, investment analysts, fund managers, financial planners, insurance agents, risk managers, consultants, and in various government positions.
- How is a B.Com (Hons) in Banking and Finance beneficial for a student?
- A Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance (Hons) provides students with a comprehensive understanding of the financial sector, preparing them for various job opportunities. The program equips students with analytical and critical thinking skills, financial knowledge, and business communication skills, making them valuable candidates in the job market. Graduates can pursue careers in banking, investment, insurance, risk management, and financial planning, among others.
- What learning outcome does a candidate have after pursuing a B.Com (Hons) in Banking and Finance?
- A candidate pursuing a B.Com (Hons) in Banking and Finance can expect to achieve learning outcomes such as understanding the principles and practices of Banking and Finance, analyzing financial statements, evaluating investment opportunities, understanding the regulatory framework, and developing skills in risk management, financial planning, and business communication. These outcomes prepare graduates for various career opportunities in the financial sector.
- What is the difference between B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance and BBA Banking and Finance?
- The main difference between B.Com (Hons) Banking and Finance and BBA Banking and Finance is that B.Com (Hons) is more focused on financial and accounting subjects, while BBA is more focused on management subjects. B.Com (Hons) emphasizes financial accounting, investment analysis, and risk management, while BBA emphasizes management principles, marketing, and organizational behavior. Both degrees prepare students for careers in the Banking and Finance industry, but the focus of the programs differs.
- The application process at Graphic Era is strictly based on the Merit of the qualifying examination with the entire Admission Process available for completion online

- B.Com. Banking and Finance is an undergraduate program that combines financial management, banking principles, and investment strategies to equip students with knowledge and skills for a career in the dynamic world of finance.
- The B.Com. Banking and Finance program is a three years program designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the banking and finance sector. It aims to equip them with the knowledge and skills to navigate the complexities of financial markets, banking operations, and investment strategies.
- Throughout the program, students gain insights into various financial instruments, market dynamics, and regulatory frameworks governing the banking sector.
- The program often includes case studies, simulations, and practical exercises to enhance students’ problem-solving and decision-making skills. It fosters analytical thinking, financial acumen, and an understanding of the ethical considerations in the finance industry.
- Additionally, students may have opportunities to engage in internships, and industry projects, or participate in workshops and seminars conducted by industry professionals. Such experiences help them develop a practical understanding of real-world banking and finance scenarios.
- Launch your career in Commerce with our B.Com. Banking and Finance program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Seats to be allocated based on projected scores(12th), calculated basis the candidate’s 10th percentage – only meritorious students
- All interested candidates should have passed Class XII from a recognized board of examination in any stream with not less than 55% marks.
- The B.Com. Banking and Finance program opens up a range of exciting career opportunities in the banking and finance sector. Graduates of this program possess the knowledge and skills required to pursue various roles in financial institutions, investment firms, insurance companies, and related industries. Some of the career paths that await B.Com. Banking and Finance graduates include:
- Banker: takes on roles such as retail banker, corporate banker, investment banker, or relationship manager in banking institutions. Responsibilities include customer transactions, account management, financial advice, and facilitating lending and investment activities.
- Financial Analyst: works as a financial analyst, analysing market trends, evaluating investment opportunities, and providing recommendations to clients or organisations. Conduct economic research, perform valuation analysis, and contribute to investment decision-making.
- Risk Manager: plays a vital role in risk management by identifying and mitigating potential risks, developing risk management strategies, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
- Investment Advisor: provides personalised investment advice to clients, assisting them in making informed decisions about their investment portfolios and financial goals.
- Financial Planner: helps individuals or businesses create comprehensive financial plans, including budgeting, retirement planning, tax strategies, and wealth management.
- Insurance specialist: works in insurance companies as an underwriter, claims specialist, or risk assessor, evaluating insurance applications, processing claims, and managing risk.
- Financial Consultant: offers expert advice on financial matters, such as investment strategies, wealth management, and financial planning.
- The everlasting importance of the B.Com. Banking and Finance program lies in its ability to provide students with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to excel in the dynamic and rewarding field of banking and finance. The following are some of the key highlights of the program-
- Practical Application: Students engage in practical exercises, case studies, and simulations to apply their knowledge to real-world scenarios, enhancing their problem-solving and decision-making skills.
- Industry-Relevant Skills: The program equips students with industry-relevant skills such as financial analysis, risk assessment, financial modelling, and market research, making them well-prepared for the demands of the banking and finance industry.
- Ethical Considerations: Students understand the ethical considerations in the finance industry, emphasising the importance of responsible and ethical practices in banking and financial operations.
- Internship Opportunities: Many programs offer internships, allowing students to gain valuable practical experience in banking and finance organisations, establishing a solid foundation for their future careers.
- Networking: Students have opportunities to connect with industry professionals and guest speakers, building a robust professional network that can facilitate career growth and open doors to job opportunities.
- Versatile Career Paths: the program offers diverse career paths in banking institutions, investment firms, insurance companies, financial consulting, and other related sectors, offering long-term growth and prospects.
- Can the B.Com. Banking and Finance program help one start their own financial business or consultancy?
- The B.Com. Banking and Finance program equips individuals with the necessary knowledge and skills to understand financial markets, manage investments, and provide financial advice. This knowledge can be applied towards starting one’s own financial business or consultancy, offering services to clients in need of financial guidance, and making it a potential pathway to entrepreneurship in the finance industry.
- How does the program stay updated with the rapidly changing banking and finance industry?
- To stay updated with the rapidly changing banking and finance industry, the program offers collaborations with industry experts, incorporates guest lectures, and regularly updates its curriculum. It integrates case studies, industry projects, and simulations to ensure students receive relevant knowledge aligned with the evolving landscape. Students stay abreast of industry trends and developments in the banking and finance sector.

- The program offers ample opportunities for practical application and internships. Students can engage in internships, industry projects, and experiential learning activities. These opportunities help students apply their theoretical knowledge, gain practical skills, build professional networks, and enhance their employability in the banking and finance field. Moreover, such projects enable students to develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and teamwork skills while addressing industry-specific issues

- Opportunities for international exposure or studying abroad are available within the B.Com. Banking and Finance program. - Master of Commerce is a two-year postgraduate program in commerce that focuses on advanced knowledge and skills in accounting, finance, economics, marketing, and management.
- The Master of Commerce (M.Com) program at Graphic Era Hill University is a two-year postgraduate program that is designed to provide students with advanced knowledge and skills in the field of commerce. The program is designed to cater to the needs of students who are looking to pursue a career in the field of finance, accounting, taxation, and business management. The program provides a strong foundation in commerce and helps students develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making skills. The program is taught by experienced faculty members who use a combination of traditional and modern teaching methods to ensure that students receive a well-rounded education.
- Launch your career in Commerce with our M.Com. program. Experienced faculty, hands-on learning, and industry connections.
- Bachelors degree in commerce/Business Administration/BA Economics or any bachelor degree who has Economics/Accountacny or Finance as a additional subject from a recoginzed university with mininum 50% marks.
- Graduates of the M.Com program at Graphic Era Hill University can pursue a wide range of careers in the field of commerce. Here are some of the popular career options for M.Com graduates:
- Accountant: An accountant is responsible for maintaining financial records, preparing financial reports, and ensuring that an organization’s financial records are accurate.
- Financial Analyst: A financial analyst is responsible for analyzing financial data and making recommendations to organizations on how to improve their financial performance.
- Tax Consultant: A tax consultant helps organizations comply with tax regulations, prepare tax returns, and provide tax planning advice.
- Business Manager: A business manager is responsible for managing the day-to-day operations of an organization, making strategic decisions, and implementing policies to ensure that an organization runs smoothly.
- Financial Planner: A financial planner helps individuals and organizations develop financial plans, manage their investments, and achieve their financial goals.
- Auditor: An auditor is responsible for evaluating an organization’s financial records and ensuring that they comply with accounting standards and regulations.
- Market Research Analyst: A market research analyst helps organizations gather and analyze data on market trends, customer behaviour, and competitive activity to develop effective marketing strategies.
- Entrepreneur: M.Com graduates can start their own businesses and use their knowledge of finance, accounting, and business management to succeed in their entrepreneurial ventures.
- Comprehensive Curriculum: The M.Com program curriculum is comprehensive and covers all aspects of commerce. The program consists of four semesters, and each semester is designed to provide students with a deeper understanding of commerce. The curriculum includes courses in accounting, finance, taxation, business management, and economics. Some of the key courses offered in the M.Com program include Financial Accounting, Business Law, Corporate Accounting, Cost Accounting, Taxation, Investment Management, and Marketing Management. The program also includes elective courses, which allow students to specialize in a particular area of commerce. The curriculum is designed to provide students with a practical and industry-oriented approach to learning. Students work on real-world projects and case studies to gain hands-on experience.
- Industry-Oriented Approach: The program is designed to provide students with a practical and industry-oriented approach to learning. Students work on real-world projects and case studies to gain hands-on experience.
- Faculty Members: The faculty members of the M.Com program are highly experienced and have a deep understanding of the commerce industry. They use a combination of traditional and modern teaching methods to ensure that students receive a well-rounded education.
- Internship Opportunities: The program provides internship opportunities to students, which help them gain practical experience and prepare them for the industry.
- Research Opportunities: Students of the M.Com program have the opportunity to participate in research projects, which help them develop their research skills and contribute to the advancement of the field of commerce.
- The course structure of M.Com (Master of Commerce) includes core subjects such as accounting, economics, business law, and management. Students also have the option to choose from a range of elective subjects such as taxation, finance, and marketing. The course also includes practical training, seminars, and a research project.
- What is the teaching methodology used in the program?
- The teaching methodology used in M.Com includes a combination of lectures, case studies, group discussions, and practical training. The lectures provide students with theoretical knowledge, while case studies and group discussions help students apply this knowledge to real-world scenarios. Practical training is also an essential component of the course, as it provides students with hands-on experience in areas such as accounting, finance, and taxation.
- What are the professional skills that a student gains from completing the course of M. Com?
- Completing the course of M.Com provides students with a range of professional skills, including accounting, financial management, business strategy, and communication. Students also gain knowledge in areas such as taxation, auditing, and corporate law. Additionally, the course emphasizes research and analytical thinking, which are essential skills for success in any business field.
- What are the challenges faced by students pursuing M. Com?
- Students pursuing M.Com face several challenges, including the need to develop a sturdy foundation in accounting and finance. The course also requires students to develop analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
